steps.step: A random sample's proportion is the best estimate for the entire population's proportion. The sample found that 20% intend to enroll, so we apply 20% to the full club of 55 students.
Verify reasonableness: 20% of 55 is slightly more than 20% of 50 = 10, so 11 is expected.

• Choice A: 11. This equals \(0.20 \times 55\), directly applying the sample proportion to the population. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: 20. This confuses the percentage value 20 with a count; 20 would only be right if there were 100 students total. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: 44. This would correspond to 80% intending to enroll (\(44/55 \approx 0.80\)), the complement of 20%. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: 55. This would mean 100% intend to enroll, contradicting the 20% sample result. Eliminated.
